elements.title: Populus Denver
elements.contents: ... that the facade of Populus Denver (pictured) has been compared to a "cheese grater"?

elements.title: Dalmatius
elements.contents: ... that Dalmatius was murdered by his soldiers during a massacre that killed nearly all the male members of the Constantinian dynasty?

elements.title: the deadliest cyclone in the Southern Hemisphere
elements.contents: ... that news of the deadliest cyclone in the Southern Hemisphere took a month to reach authorities?

elements.title: Israel Keyes
elements.contents: ... that Israel Keyes buried "murder kits", containing weapons and supplies, years before using them in his crimes?

elements.title: Drill Dozer
elements.contents: ... that the character designer for Drill Dozer had his wife model with two roll cakes on her head to create the main character's hair?

elements.title: Harold Orlob
elements.contents: ... that Harold Orlob was not credited as a composer of the 1909 hit song "I Wonder Who's Kissing Her Now" until he sued in 1947?

elements.title: The Mosquito Bowl
elements.contents: ... that The Mosquito Bowl is based on a college football game played before the battle of Okinawa?

elements.title: Adelaide Ironside
elements.contents: ... that a painting by Adelaide Ironside modelled its depiction of Christ on the Italian general Giuseppe Garibaldi?

elements.title: High Bridge
elements.contents: ... that the commissioner of the New York City Department of Bridges did not classify the High Bridge as a bridge?
